# UC-23.2.5 · Pricing and Discount Effectiveness Analysis

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Platform &middot; **Type:** Business &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We group closed deals by how big the markdown was and check whether those markdowns usually went hand-in-hand with wins. When teams cut prices steeply but still lose often, we say money walked out the door; when modest trims keep wins flowing, pricing stays sensible.*

---

## Description

Measures discount-band win-rate and dollar concession on Closed Won versus Closed Lost Salesforce opportunities ingested via DB Connect or line-item feeds, derives missing `discount_pct` from list minus booked amount, assigns bands from no-discount through over fifty percent, rolls up portfolio totals with `eventstats`, labels each band Effective versus Margin erosion versus Ineffective using explicit RevOps thresholds, and optionally attaches closed-deal rep cardinality for CPQ guardrail reviews.

## Value

RevOps and pricing councils finally see whether deeper concessions correlate with wins or merely bleed gross margin — by band, not anecdotes — which informs CPQ approval tiers, SPIFs, and regional pricebook tuning without waiting for quarterly FP&A elasticity studies. When bands above thirty percent win-rate crater yet dollar concessions climb, leadership intervenes on discount culture before EBITDA slips in board packs; when shallow discounts accompany healthy win-rate, quota-bearing reps retain autonomy instead of blanket bans.

## Implementation

Land Opportunity plus OpportunityLineItem or CPQ quote-line replicas into `index=business`; schedule DB Connect pulls every four hours with rising keys on `LastModifiedDate`; publish Splunk lookups for product-master list refreshes when Finance rebases catalog pricing; paste the SPL into a Dashboard Studio workbook with fiscal-quarter tokens and route Margin erosion slices to Pricing Ops plus Sales FP&A weekly.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Heavy Forwarder hosting Splunk DB Connect 3.x/4.x with JDBC paths into Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, or Salesforce replica schemas; outbound TLS to Splunk indexers on `8089/tcp` or Cloud ingest endpoints.
- Salesforce integration credentials scoped read-only on Opportunity, OpportunityLineItem, PricebookEntry, Quote, QuoteLineItem, plus whichever CPQ objects your tenant activates (`SBQQ__Quote__c`, `SBQQ__QuoteLine__c`, Apttus proposal tables, Conga adjustment tables).
- Splunk roles granting `srchIndexesAllowed` on `business` and optionally `summary` if snapshotting weekly band aggregates.
- FP&A alignment on whether Opportunity `amount` represents ACV, TCV, or multi-year prepaid totals — document the ruling beside this UC because derived `discount_pct` inherits that semantic.
- Reference benchmarks handy for calibration panels (cross-industry B2B win-rate roughly twenty-one percent; SaaS cohorts often twenty-eight to thirty-four percent) without coding them into SPL unless governance mandates hard stops.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
Stand up DB Connect identities per warehouse vendor; schedule inputs so CRM snapshots arrive at least hourly for tactical pricing meetings yet throttle Bulk extracts under Salesforce fifty-thousand-row aggregation limits by slicing SOQL or SQL predicates on `CloseDate` / `LastModifiedDate`. Typical JDBC selects union Opportunity headers with exploded line rows:

```sql
SELECT o.id AS opportunity_id,
       o.stagename,
       o.amount,
       oli.listprice,
       oli.discount,
       oli.totalprice,
       oli.quantity
FROM salesforce.opportunity o
LEFT JOIN salesforce.opportunitylineitem oli ON oli.opportunityid = o.id
WHERE o.isclosed = TRUE
  AND o.closedate >= CURRENT_DATE - 540
```

Map vendor column names to Splunk-friendly keys (`discount_pct`, `list_price`, `amount`) inside the input or via `FIELDALIAS`. For Salesforce CPQ Steelbrick, replicate `SBQQ__QuoteLine__c` with `SBQQ__AdditionalDiscount__c` and `SBQQ__PartnerDiscount__c` separated so Splunk can later separate rep vs partner motion. For Apttus, stream `Apttus_QPApprov__Approval__c` when legal requires alert routing whenever `discount_pct` exceeds workflow thresholds even if statistical bands look fine. Emit sourcetype `dbx:crm_opportunities` for wide denormalized rows; if you ingest skinny line feeds, union them in SPL with shared `OppKey` as shown in Step 2. Set `_time` from `CloseDate` for closed snapshots or `LastModifiedDate` if you track intra-quarter repricing — document the choice because discount effectiveness trends move when the clock anchor changes.

### Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Use the SPL from the top-level `spl` field verbatim in Search, then save as `pricing_band_effectiveness_qtr` before promoting to Dashboard Studio. Wire a scheduled report every Monday 06:00 local for regional reviews, and clone a second saved search with `where match(verdict,"Margin erosion.*")` feeding a Teams webhook — not a page unless your culture treats pricing discipline as Sev-1.

```spl
index=business (sourcetype="dbx:crm_opportunities" OR sourcetype="dbx:quote_line_item" OR sourcetype="salesforce:opportunity_lineitem") earliest=-180d@d latest=now()
| eval OppKey=coalesce(Id, id, opportunity_id, OppId, sfdc_id)
| eval StageName=coalesce(StageName, stage_name)
| eval amount=tonumber(coalesce(Amount, amount, TotalPrice, "0"))
| eval list_price=tonumber(coalesce(ListPrice, list_price, SBQQ__ListPrice__c, "0"))
| eval discount_pct=if(isnotnull(discount_pct), discount_pct, round(100*(list_price-amount)/list_price, 1))
| where like(StageName,"%Closed Won%") OR like(StageName,"%Closed Lost%")
| eval is_won=if(like(StageName,"%Closed Won%") OR match(lower(tostring(coalesce(IsWon,is_won,""))),"^(1|true)$"),1,0)
| eval discount_band=case(isnull(discount_pct) OR discount_pct<=0,"No Discount",discount_pct<=10,"1-10%",discount_pct<=20,"11-20%",discount_pct<=30,"21-30%",discount_pct<=50,"31-50%",1=1,">50%")
| eval dollar_concession=if(list_price>0,list_price-amount,null())
| stats count as deals sum(is_won) as wins avg(amount) as avg_deal_size avg(discount_pct) as avg_discount avg(dollar_concession) as avg_dollar_concession perc95(amount) as p95_deal_size by discount_band
| eval win_rate=if(deals>0, round(100*wins/deals, 1), null())
| eventstats sum(deals) as portfolio_deals sum(wins) as portfolio_wins
| eval portfolio_win_rate=if(portfolio_deals>0, round(100*portfolio_wins/portfolio_deals, 1), null())
| eval verdict=case(win_rate > 50 AND avg_discount < 15, "Effective", win_rate < 30, "Ineffective — discount not winning", avg_discount > 25, "Margin erosion — high discount low effectiveness", 1=1, "Mixed")
| appendcols [ search index=business sourcetype="dbx:crm_opportunities" earliest=-180d@d latest=now() | eval StageName=coalesce(StageName, stage_name) | where like(StageName,"%Closed%") | eval OwnerId=coalesce(OwnerId, owner_id, sales_rep_id) | stats dc(OwnerId) as distinct_closed_rep_rows ]
| sort - win_rate
| table discount_band deals wins win_rate avg_deal_size avg_discount avg_dollar_concession p95_deal_size portfolio_deals portfolio_win_rate verdict distinct_closed_rep_rows
```

Alert suggestion: trigger when any row shows verdict matching Margin erosion while `deals>=30` and `avg_deal_size` exceeds portfolio `p95_deal_size` — indicative of whales distorting aggregate bands.

### Step 3 — Pipeline mechanics
The initial filter restricts `index=business` sourcetypes carrying CRM economics. `eval` harmonizes heterogeneous CRM keys into `OppKey`, aligns casing drift between REST exports and JDBC warehouses, then derives amount and list references before computing missing percentages exactly per Finance preference: reuse populated `discount_pct` whenever trust exists; otherwise infer using list minus booked numerator guarded by list price in the denominator. Stage filters retain only exited pipeline outcomes because discount effectiveness answers retrospective pricing discipline — mixing open stages would contaminate win-rate denominators. `is_won` prefers Closed Won naming conventions aligned with Salesforce reporting yet still respects boolean mirrors when incremental feeds omit verbose stages. Banding buckets follow RevOps-ready ladders through fifty percent then funnel extremes above that mark into the top bucket — tweak thresholds via macros if your governance stops at forty-five percent VP approval.
Aggregation collapses repeated line-ingest explosions into discount strata using `stats`, preserving weighted averages through Splunk’s implicit handling per band. Concession averages quantify dollar bleed independent of percentages — crucial when comparing hardware-heavy deals against SaaS bundles.
`eventstats` layers portfolio denominators atop each strata row without collapsing bands so Effective versus Margin erosion judgments compare band-local win-rate against shared organizational totals.
Optional `appendcols` brings a lightweight denominator describing distinct reps touching closed outcomes — not a substitute for full Owner-level clustering but enough to warn when verdict drift stems from one superstar versus systemic pricing leakage.
Closing `sort` and `table` format RevOps-ready worksheets.

### Step 4 — Validate
Pick seven closed Opportunities spanning each discount band directly inside Salesforce Lightning record pages — compare Amount, rollup line totals, CPQ Net Price fields, and Stage labels against Splunk rows keyed by `OppKey`. Confirm against the Salesforce Reports dashboard that rolled-up closed-won dollars match Splunk totals Finance stamps each quarter.
Open the Sales Reporting tab Run Quote Reports tied to Steelbrick objects when CPQ feeds partially hydrate warehouse tables.
Within Splunk, run `stats count by sourcetype` to confirm JDBC versus TA blends remain balanced — sudden dominance of one sourcetype hints at broken incremental extracts doubling counts.
For numeric parity, reconcile Splunk `sum(amount)` closed-won totals versus Salesforce cumulative Closed Won dashboards filtered on the identical fiscal quarter — variance typically traces omitted enterprise Ultimate-tier Pricebook rows or sandbox refreshes leaking QA Amount figures.
Stress-test derived discounts by forcing rows where Salesforce exposes List equals Amount yet CPQ logged discretionary concessions solely inside Quote Lines — such mismatches justify enriching ingest with CPQ-specific discount columns highlighted in Step 1.

### Step 5 — Operational caveats and known false positives
- Finance refreshes catalog list_price mid-quarter without versioning dashboards — every downstream derived percentage shifts simultaneously; mitigation tags rows with `pricebook_version_id` when warehouses expose it.
- Mixed bundles allocate steep reductions on dormant SKUs while core platform SKUs remain nearly full price — Opportunity-level averages imply Margin erosion though each SKU respected approval matrices.
- Strategic pilots priced at zero dollars for marquee logos inflate win-rate inside extreme bands though finance booked ARR elsewhere.
- EUR versus USD versus APAC lists diverge via FX rounding — identical Splunk percentages yield unlike pocket margins after treasury translation.
- Channel-heavy quotes subtract distributor allowances already baked into payable `amount`, overstating ostensible Salesforce discounts versus internal list comparisons.
- Fiscal calendars concentrate Executive-approved concessions inside final-week pushes — Margin erosion verdicts spike episodically versus steady-state selling weeks.
- Representatives occasionally park legitimate wins under Closed Lost briefly near fiscal fences to manipulate quota timing — discount-band win-rate dips temporarily though pricing behavior never changed (cross-check Owner narratives rather than trusting Stage alone).
- Multi-year prepayment captures five-year TCV inside Opportunity Amount while line lists Year-One SKU pricing — inferred percentages scream leakage unless Splunk joins ramp schedules from CPQ amendment tables.
- Troubleshooting ingest: warehouse joins occasionally omit PricebookEntry bridges — missing fields make inferred percentages absurd until upstream COALESCE restores catalogue defaults.
- Troubleshooting throughput: Salesforce Bulk API 429 responses appear during busy extracts — stale Splunk feeds flatten win-rate curves until concurrency backoff completes.

## SPL

```spl
index=business (sourcetype="dbx:crm_opportunities" OR sourcetype="dbx:quote_line_item" OR sourcetype="salesforce:opportunity_lineitem") earliest=-180d@d latest=now()
| eval OppKey=coalesce(Id, id, opportunity_id, OppId, sfdc_id)
| eval StageName=coalesce(StageName, stage_name)
| eval amount=tonumber(coalesce(Amount, amount, TotalPrice, "0"))
| eval list_price=tonumber(coalesce(ListPrice, list_price, SBQQ__ListPrice__c, "0"))
| eval discount_pct=if(isnotnull(discount_pct), discount_pct, round(100*(list_price-amount)/list_price, 1))
| where like(StageName,"%Closed Won%") OR like(StageName,"%Closed Lost%")
| eval is_won=if(like(StageName,"%Closed Won%") OR match(lower(tostring(coalesce(IsWon,is_won,""))),"^(1|true)$"),1,0)
| eval discount_band=case(isnull(discount_pct) OR discount_pct<=0,"No Discount",discount_pct<=10,"1-10%",discount_pct<=20,"11-20%",discount_pct<=30,"21-30%",discount_pct<=50,"31-50%",1=1,">50%")
| eval dollar_concession=if(list_price>0,list_price-amount,null())
| stats count as deals sum(is_won) as wins avg(amount) as avg_deal_size avg(discount_pct) as avg_discount avg(dollar_concession) as avg_dollar_concession perc95(amount) as p95_deal_size by discount_band
| eval win_rate=if(deals>0, round(100*wins/deals, 1), null())
| eventstats sum(deals) as portfolio_deals sum(wins) as portfolio_wins
| eval portfolio_win_rate=if(portfolio_deals>0, round(100*portfolio_wins/portfolio_deals, 1), null())
| eval verdict=case(win_rate > 50 AND avg_discount < 15, "Effective", win_rate < 30, "Ineffective — discount not winning", avg_discount > 25, "Margin erosion — high discount low effectiveness", 1=1, "Mixed")
| appendcols [ search index=business sourcetype="dbx:crm_opportunities" earliest=-180d@d latest=now() | eval StageName=coalesce(StageName, stage_name) | where like(StageName,"%Closed%") | eval OwnerId=coalesce(OwnerId, owner_id, sales_rep_id) | stats dc(OwnerId) as distinct_closed_rep_rows ]
| sort - win_rate
| table discount_band deals wins win_rate avg_deal_size avg_discount avg_dollar_concession p95_deal_size portfolio_deals portfolio_win_rate verdict distinct_closed_rep_rows
```

## Visualization

Dashboard Studio: stacked bar of won vs lost counts by `discount_band`; single value for `portfolio_win_rate` with prior-quarter overlay; heatmap of `verdict` by `discount_band` and theater; scatter of `avg_dollar_concession` vs `win_rate` with bubble size `p95_deal_size`; trellis table for `discount_band` with drilldown to Salesforce `OpportunityId` lists; optional line chart of weekly `avg_discount` for EOQ surge monitoring.

## Known False Positives

A single **list-price uplift** pushed at week five of the quarter silently re-bases every downstream `discount_pct` even when deal points did not move, so Margin-erosion alarms spike without behavioral change until Finance publishes both old and new pricebook IDs side by side. **Multi-product bundles** frequently carry five percent haircut on commodity SKUs alongside forty percent on shelfware in the same Opportunity — averaging to twenty-two percent trips the Margin erosion verdict while each line obeyed policy. **Loss-leader whale hunts** record near-one-hundred percent concession on pilots intentionally structured as paid pilots elsewhere on paper, lifting win-rate inside the highest discount band without repeatable economics. **Regional pricebooks** (USD versus EUR versus JPY lists at PAR FX snapshots) mean identical Salesforce percentages erase different pocket-margin outcomes — Splunk sees percentage, not treasury-adjusted contribution. **Partner-led resale** injects distributor margin already carved out of net `amount`, mimicking thirty-plus percent discounts versus corporate list though revenue-share cleared Deal Desk. **End-of-quarter surge approvals** cluster inside the final ten days and inflate average concession versus quiet mid-quarter weeks independent of seller skill. **Ramp or prepaid multi-year constructs** sometimes stuff five-year TCV into Opportunity `amount` while list references Year-One SKU rows — computed `discount_pct` looks wildly generous versus Year-One ARR truth. **Free professional-services bundles** negotiated verbally shrink realized ASP yet rarely capture into CPQ-derived percentage fields. Each scenario requires pairing Splunk bands with ERP gross-margin proxies before withholding payouts.
